title: Getting Started with comma
text: comma (Comparative Methylomics Microbial Analysis) R package genome-wide analysis bacterial DNA methylation Oxford Nanopore sequencing data. supports three modification types — N6-methyladenine (6mA), 5-methylcytosine (5mC), N4-methylcytosine (4mC) — single, unified data container. vignette walks complete analysis workflow using built-comma_example_data synthetic dataset. typical comma workflow five steps: Load per-sample methylation files commaData object. QC data (coverage, beta distributions, PCA). Annotate sites relative genomic features. Test differential methylation conditions. Visualize results.

title: Getting Started with comma
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install("comma") library(comma)

title: Getting Started with comma
text: commaData extends SummarizedExperiment central data container comma. stores: methylation — sites × samples matrix beta values (0–1). coverage — sites × samples matrix read depths. rowData — per-site metadata: chromosome, position, strand, mod_type. colData — per-sample metadata: sample_name, condition, replicate. genomeInfo — chromosome names lengths. annotation — genomic features GRanges object. motifSites — motif instances GRanges object. built-comma_example_data contains 300 synthetic methylation sites (200 × 6mA, 100 × 5mC) simulated 100 kb chromosome across three samples: two controls (ctrl_1, ctrl_2) one treatment (treat_1).
code: data(comma_example_data) comma_example_data #> class: commaData #> sites: 588 | samples: 6  #> mod types: 5mC, 6mA  #> motifs: CCWGG, GATC  #> mod contexts: 5mC_CCWGG, 6mA_GATC  #> conditions: control, treatment  #> genome: 1 chromosome (100,000 bp total)  #> annotation: 5 features  #> motif sites: none # Modification types present modTypes(comma_example_data) #> [1] "5mC" "6mA"  # Per-sample metadata sampleInfo(comma_example_data) #>         sample_name condition replicate caller #> ctrl_1       ctrl_1   control         1 modkit #> ctrl_2       ctrl_2   control         2 modkit #> ctrl_3       ctrl_3   control         3 modkit #> treat_1     treat_1 treatment         1 modkit #> treat_2     treat_2 treatment         2 modkit #> treat_3     treat_3 treatment         3 modkit  # Matrix dimensions: sites × samples dim(methylation(comma_example_data)) #> [1] 588   6

title: Getting Started with comma
text: methylomeSummary() returns tidy data frame per-sample distribution statistics:
code: ms <- methylomeSummary(comma_example_data) ms[, c("sample_name", "condition", "mean_beta", "median_beta", "n_covered")] #>   sample_name condition mean_beta median_beta n_covered #> 1      ctrl_1   control 0.8654839   0.8929141       588 #> 2      ctrl_2   control 0.8705692   0.8959600       588 #> 3      ctrl_3   control 0.8638033   0.8918851       588 #> 4     treat_1 treatment 0.8357998   0.8864176       588 #> 5     treat_2 treatment 0.8369054   0.8893089       588 #> 6     treat_3 treatment 0.8388398   0.8866568       588

title: Getting Started with comma
text: plot_coverage() shows distribution sequencing depth per site, per sample. Consistent coverage across samples important quality indicator. Coverage depth distribution per sample.
code: plot_coverage(comma_example_data)

title: Getting Started with comma
text: plot_methylation_distribution() plots density methylation levels sample. Bacterial genomes often show bimodal distribution (sites either fully methylated unmethylated). Methylation beta value density per sample, faceted modification type. Restrict single modification type: Beta value density 6mA sites .
code: plot_methylation_distribution(comma_example_data) plot_methylation_distribution(comma_example_data, mod_type = "6mA")

title: Getting Started with comma
text: plot_pca() performs PCA per-sample methylation profiles. Samples condition cluster together. Internally, beta values converted M-values via mValues() PCA, stabilizes variance across sites near 0 1. PCA methylation profiles colored condition. retrieve underlying scores custom plotting, use return_data = TRUE. result data.frame PC1, PC2, sample metadata columns; percentage variance explained PC stored attr(result, "percentVar").
code: plot_pca(comma_example_data, color_by = "condition") pca_df <- plot_pca(comma_example_data, return_data = TRUE) attr(pca_df, "percentVar")  # variance explained by PC1 and PC2 #>  PC1  PC2  #> 36.3 17.4

title: Getting Started with comma
text: annotateSites() maps methylation sites genomic features, always computing four parallel list columns rowData: feature_types — GFF3 feature type association (e.g., "gene", "promoter"). feature_names — feature name association. rel_position — signed distance feature (0 = inside; negative = upstream; positive = downstream, strand-aware). frac_position — normalized position within feature ([0, 1]; NA sites outside). Use keep argument filter associations retained: "" (default), "overlap" (overlapping features), "proximity" (retains rel_position, drops frac_position), "metagene" (overlapping features frac_position). plot_metagene() visualizes average methylation profile across gene bodies: Mean methylation profile across gene bodies (TSS TTS).
code: annotated <- annotateSites(comma_example_data) si <- siteInfo(annotated)  # Proportion of sites overlapping at least one annotated feature mean(lengths(si$feature_names) > 0) #> [1] 0.03401361 plot_metagene(comma_example_data, feature = "gene")

title: Getting Started with comma
text: plot_genome_track() produces genome browser–style plot methylation along chromosome region: Genome track first 50 kb chr_sim.
code: plot_genome_track(comma_example_data, chromosome = "chr_sim",                   start = 1L, end = 50000L, mod_type = "6mA")

title: Getting Started with comma
text: diffMethyl() tests site differential methylation conditions. modeled DESeq2’s workflow: pass commaData object design formula, receive back object statistical results rowData. Extract results tidy data frame: Filter significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2):
code: cd_dm <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition,                     mod_type = "6mA") cd_dm #> class: commaData #> sites: 588 | samples: 6  #> mod types: 5mC, 6mA  #> motifs: CCWGG, GATC  #> mod contexts: 5mC_CCWGG, 6mA_GATC  #> conditions: control, treatment  #> genome: 1 chromosome (100,000 bp total)  #> annotation: 5 features  #> motif sites: none res <- results(cd_dm) # Top sites by adjusted p-value head(res[order(res$dm_padj),          c("chrom", "position", "mod_type", "dm_delta_beta", "dm_padj")]) #>                            chrom position mod_type dm_delta_beta      dm_padj #> chr_sim:50176:-:6mA:GATC chr_sim    50176      6mA    -0.7336497 1.849154e-75 #> chr_sim:70003:-:6mA:GATC chr_sim    70003      6mA    -0.7050844 3.896483e-68 #> chr_sim:63550:+:6mA:GATC chr_sim    63550      6mA    -0.7799241 5.006897e-66 #> chr_sim:61440:+:6mA:GATC chr_sim    61440      6mA    -0.7090099 1.178364e-64 #> chr_sim:86016:+:6mA:GATC chr_sim    86016      6mA    -0.6743832 3.661541e-62 #> chr_sim:2180:-:6mA:GATC  chr_sim     2180      6mA    -0.7543758 4.024452e-60 sig <- filterResults(cd_dm, padj = 0.05, delta_beta = 0.2) cat("Significant sites:", nrow(sig), "\n") #> Significant sites: 31

title: Getting Started with comma
text: plot_volcano() displays differential methylation landscape. Sites colored direction significance: Volcano plot: effect size (Δβ) vs. significance (–log10 padj).
code: plot_volcano(res)

title: Getting Started with comma
text: plot_heatmap() shows methylation beta values top differentially methylated sites: Heatmap top 30 differentially methylated 6mA sites.
code: plot_heatmap(res, cd_dm, n_sites = 30L)

title: Working with Multiple Modification Types
text: Bacterial genomes frequently carry multiple simultaneous DNA methylation marks: 6mA (N6-methyladenine) — deposited Dam methyltransferase GATC motifs; functions mismatch repair cell-cycle regulation. 5mC (5-methylcytosine) — deposited Dcm methyltransferase CCWGG motifs; roles gene regulation less well characterized bacteria. 4mC (N4-methylcytosine) — deposited restriction-modification systems various sequence motifs; protects foreign DNA. comma stores modification types single commaData object, enabling joint analysis, comparison, visualization across modification types. vignette demonstrates multi-modification workflows using built-comma_example_data dataset, contains 6mA 5mC sites.
code: library(comma) data(comma_example_data)

title: Working with Multiple Modification Types
code: modTypes(comma_example_data) #> [1] "5mC" "6mA"

title: Working with Multiple Modification Types
code: table(siteInfo(comma_example_data)$mod_type) #>  #> 5mC 6mA  #> 195 393

title: Working with Multiple Modification Types
text: methylomeSummary() reports stats per sample. Combine mod_type filtering compare distributions:
code: methylomeSummary(comma_example_data, mod_type = "6mA")[,     c("sample_name", "condition", "mean_beta", "median_beta", "n_covered")] #>   sample_name condition mean_beta median_beta n_covered #> 1      ctrl_1   control 0.9036813   0.9173248       393 #> 2      ctrl_2   control 0.9029957   0.9139209       393 #> 3      ctrl_3   control 0.9014299   0.9133574       393 #> 4     treat_1 treatment 0.8557059   0.9107859       393 #> 5     treat_2 treatment 0.8491166   0.9039562       393 #> 6     treat_3 treatment 0.8527682   0.9074161       393 methylomeSummary(comma_example_data, mod_type = "5mC")[,     c("sample_name", "condition", "mean_beta", "median_beta", "n_covered")] #>   sample_name condition mean_beta median_beta n_covered #> 1      ctrl_1   control 0.7885015   0.8101719       195 #> 2      ctrl_2   control 0.8052173   0.8270815       195 #> 3      ctrl_3   control 0.7879713   0.8214410       195 #> 4     treat_1 treatment 0.7956813   0.8264400       195 #> 5     treat_2 treatment 0.8122951   0.8483001       195 #> 6     treat_3 treatment 0.8107686   0.8296301       195

title: Working with Multiple Modification Types
text: plot_methylation_distribution() automatically facets modification type multiple types present: Beta value density per sample, faceted modification type. 6mA sites bacteria commonly show bimodal distribution (near 0 near 1) restriction-modification systems methylate recognition sequences high efficiency. 5mC patterns can variable.
code: plot_methylation_distribution(comma_example_data, per_sample = TRUE)

title: Working with Multiple Modification Types
text: Use subset() extract single modification type:
code: only_6ma <- subset(comma_example_data, mod_type = "6mA") only_5mc <- subset(comma_example_data, mod_type = "5mC")  cat("6mA sites:", nrow(methylation(only_6ma)), "\n") #> 6mA sites: 393 cat("5mC sites:", nrow(methylation(only_5mc)), "\n") #> 5mC sites: 195

previous_headings: Differential Methylation: Testing Each Modification Type
what: Test all types in one call
title: Working with Multiple Modification Types
text: default, diffMethyl() tests modification types present object. Use results(..., mod_type = "6mA") extract type-specific results:
code: dm_all <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition) res_6ma <- results(dm_all, mod_type = "6mA") res_5mc <- results(dm_all, mod_type = "5mC")  cat("6mA: significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2):",     sum(!is.na(res_6ma$dm_padj) & res_6ma$dm_padj < 0.05 &         abs(res_6ma$dm_delta_beta) >= 0.2, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> 6mA: significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2): 31 cat("5mC: significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2):",     sum(!is.na(res_5mc$dm_padj) & res_5mc$dm_padj < 0.05 &         abs(res_5mc$dm_delta_beta) >= 0.2, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> 5mC: significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2): 11

previous_headings: Differential Methylation: Testing Each Modification Type
title: Working with Multiple Modification Types
text: 6mA differential methylation (top) 5mC (bottom).
code: p_6ma <- plot_volcano(res_6ma) +     ggplot2::ggtitle("6mA differential methylation") p_5mc <- plot_volcano(res_5mc) +     ggplot2::ggtitle("5mC differential methylation")  # Arrange side by side if patchwork is available if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) {     patchwork::wrap_plots(p_6ma, p_5mc, ncol = 1L) } else {     print(p_6ma)     print(p_5mc) }

title: Working with Multiple Modification Types
text: plot_pca() converts beta values M-values via mValues() PCA, stabilizes variance sites near 0 1. default sites used; pass mod_type restrict one modification type: PCA using modification types. PCA using 6mA sites . Use return_data = TRUE retrieve PC scores custom plots downstream analysis:
code: plot_pca(comma_example_data, color_by = "condition") plot_pca(comma_example_data, mod_type = "6mA", color_by = "condition") pca_df <- plot_pca(comma_example_data, mod_type = "6mA", return_data = TRUE) # data.frame with PC1, PC2, sample_name, condition, replicate, ... head(pca_df[, c("sample_name", "condition", "PC1", "PC2")]) #>   sample_name condition       PC1         PC2 #> 1      ctrl_1   control -15.40587  11.1961851 #> 2      ctrl_2   control -15.28274   5.0977438 #> 3      ctrl_3   control -16.11112 -14.9438670 #> 4     treat_1 treatment  13.82789  -8.7254793 #> 5     treat_2 treatment  15.15304   0.4254472 #> 6     treat_3 treatment  17.81880   6.9499703 attr(pca_df, "percentVar") #>  PC1  PC2  #> 44.4 15.1

title: comma: for microbial methylation analysis
text: comma (comma: microbial methylation analysis) R package genome-wide analysis bacterial DNA methylation Oxford Nanopore sequencing data. accepts output modkit, Dorado, Megalodon, supports 6mA, 5mC, 4mC single unified container, covers full analysis workflow raw files quality control, site annotation, differential methylation testing, publication-quality visualization.

title: comma: for microbial methylation analysis
text: commaData S4 class — extends SummarizedExperiment; stores beta values, coverage, sample metadata, genome info, annotations together. Three parsers — modkit pileup BED (primary), Dorado BAM (full MM/ML tag decoding), Megalodon (legacy). Vectorized annotation via GenomicRanges::findOverlaps(); three modes: overlap, proximity, metagene (normalized position within feature bodies). Differential methylation — quasibinomial GLM per site DESeq2-style interface (diffMethyl() → results() → filterResults()). Seven plot_*() functions — coverage QC, beta distributions, PCA, genome tracks, metagene profiles, volcano plots, heatmaps; return ggplot objects customization. PCA uses variance-stabilized M-values internally supports return_data = TRUE custom plotting. mValues() — converts beta values read depths M-values (log2((M + α) / (U + α))), useful distance-based analyses custom plots. bacterial genome — organism-specific values hardcoded anywhere. Multi-modification-type — 6mA, 5mC, 4mC coexist one object; every function accepts mod_type argument work individual types.

title: comma: for microbial methylation analysis
text: commaData extends SummarizedExperiment constructed per-sample methylation files: built-synthetic dataset (300 sites, 3 samples, 100 kb genome) included testing demonstrations:
code: library(comma)  # Named vector: sample_name → path to modkit pileup BED files <- c(   ctrl_1  = "path/to/ctrl_rep1.bed",   ctrl_2  = "path/to/ctrl_rep2.bed",   treat_1 = "path/to/treat_rep1.bed",   treat_2 = "path/to/treat_rep2.bed" )  col_data <- data.frame(   sample_name = c("ctrl_1",    "ctrl_2",    "treat_1",    "treat_2"),   condition   = c("control",   "control",   "treatment", "treatment"),   replicate   = c(1L,          2L,          1L,          2L) )  obj <- commaData(   files        = files,   colData      = col_data,   genome       = "path/to/genome.fa",   # FASTA, BSgenome, or named integer vector   annotation   = "path/to/genes.gff3",  # GFF3 or BED (optional)   motif        = "GATC",                # regex motif (optional)   min_coverage = 5L,   caller       = "modkit"               # "modkit", "dorado", or "megalodon" ) library(comma) data(comma_example_data) comma_example_data #> class: commaData #> sites: 300 | samples: 6  #> mod types: 5mC, 6mA  #> conditions: control, treatment  #> genome: 1 chromosome (100,000 bp total)  #> annotation: 5 features  #> motif sites: none

title: comma: for microbial methylation analysis
text: methylomeSummary() returns per-sample distribution statistics: plot_coverage() shows sequencing depth distribution per sample:  plot_methylation_distribution() plots density beta values, faceted modification type. Bacterial methylomes often show bimodal distribution (sites either fully methylated unmethylated):  plot_pca() runs PCA per-sample methylation profiles sample-level QC. Beta values converted M-values PCA better variance stabilization. Use return_data = TRUE retrieve scores data frame custom plotting:
code: ms <- methylomeSummary(comma_example_data) ms[, c("sample_name", "condition", "mean_beta", "median_beta",        "frac_methylated", "n_covered")] #>   sample_name condition mean_beta median_beta frac_methylated n_covered #> 1      ctrl_1   control 0.8678843   0.8881436       0.9900000       300 #> 2      ctrl_2   control 0.8728354   0.8951648       0.9966667       300 #> 3      ctrl_3   control 0.8781476   0.8966108       1.0000000       300 #> 4     treat_1 treatment 0.8135452   0.8829561       0.9166667       300 #> 5     treat_2 treatment 0.8136529   0.8867238       0.9000000       300 #> 6     treat_3 treatment 0.8004998   0.8694701       0.9066667       300 plot_coverage(comma_example_data) plot_methylation_distribution(comma_example_data) plot_pca(comma_example_data, color_by = "condition") pca_df <- plot_pca(comma_example_data, return_data = TRUE) attr(pca_df, "percentVar")  # variance explained by PC1, PC2

title: comma: for microbial methylation analysis
text: annotateSites() maps sites genomic features using GenomicRanges::findOverlaps() — vectorized, nested loops: Three annotation modes available: "overlap", "proximity" (nearest feature signed distance), "metagene" (normalized position 0–1 within feature bodies). plot_metagene() shows average methylation profile across gene bodies:
code: annotated <- annotateSites(comma_example_data, type = "overlap")  # Fraction of sites overlapping at least one annotated feature si <- siteInfo(annotated) mean(lengths(si$feature_names) > 0) #> [1] 0.02333333 plot_metagene(comma_example_data, feature = "gene")

title: comma: for microbial methylation analysis

code: plot_genome_track(comma_example_data, chromosome = "chr_sim",                   start = 1L, end = 50000L, mod_type = "6mA")

title: comma: for microbial methylation analysis
text: diffMethyl() tests site differential methylation conditions. modeled DESeq2’s workflow: pass commaData object design formula, get back object per-site statistics rowData: Extract results tidy data frame filter significant sites: plot_volcano() displays differential methylation landscape — effect size (delta methylation) versus significance (–log10 padj):  plot_heatmap() shows per-sample beta values top differentially methylated sites:
code: cd_dm <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition,                     mod_type = "6mA") res <- results(cd_dm) sig <- filterResults(cd_dm, padj = 0.05, delta_beta = 0.2) cat("Total 6mA sites tested:", nrow(res), "\n") #> Total 6mA sites tested: 300 cat("Significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2):", nrow(sig), "\n") #> Significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2): 7  # Top hits head(res[order(res$dm_padj),          c("chrom", "position", "dm_delta_beta", "dm_padj")]) #>                       chrom position dm_delta_beta     dm_padj #> chr_sim:8907:-:6mA  chr_sim     8907    -0.6097199 0.009737468 #> chr_sim:52014:+:6mA chr_sim    52014    -0.7591100 0.009737468 #> chr_sim:69527:+:6mA chr_sim    69527    -0.6702704 0.009737468 #> chr_sim:72824:-:6mA chr_sim    72824    -0.1353157 0.009737468 #> chr_sim:62293:-:6mA chr_sim    62293    -0.6522237 0.012869199 #> chr_sim:9028:-:6mA  chr_sim     9028    -0.6850134 0.018361742 plot_volcano(res) plot_heatmap(res, cd_dm, n_sites = 30L)

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
text: object commaData object. features GRanges genomic features annotate . NULL (default), annotation stored object via annotation(object) used. Must mcols columns named feature_col name_col. feature_col Character string. Name mcols column features contains feature type. Default: "feature_type". name_col Character string. Name mcols column features contains feature name. Default: "name". window Integer. Search window base pairs. features within distance site returned. Default: 50L. keep Character string controlling output columns retained: "" (default) Return four columns associations — including sites within window inside feature. "overlap" Subset site's associations features rel_position == 0 (inside feature). Drop rel_position frac_position columns. "proximity" Keep associations. Drop frac_position column. "metagene" Subset rel_position == 0. Drop rel_position column. Retain frac_position. metadata_cols Character vector NULL. Names additional mcols columns features pass parallel list columns rowData. column X stored X_values (CharacterList). Default: NULL.

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
text: commaData object identical object except rowData extended annotation list columns. keep = "" (default): feature_types CharacterList. Feature type association per site. feature_names CharacterList. Feature name association per site. rel_position IntegerList. Signed relative position (bp): 0 inside, negative upstream, positive downstream. frac_position NumericList. Fractional position \([0, 1]\) inside features; NA outside. Intergenic sites (features within window) receive length-0 list elements columns.

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
text: function searches within window bp site returns every feature found. Four parallel list columns added rowData: feature types, feature names, signed relative position, fractional position within feature. Signed rel_position convention (strand-aware): 0 — site inside feature. negative — site upstream (feature direction transcription). positive — site downstream (feature). frac_position \([0, 1]\) site inside feature (TSS = 0, TTS = 1, strand-aware) NA site outside. list columns strictly parallel: element \(j\) feature_types[[]] corresponds element \(j\) feature_names[[]], rel_position[[]], frac_position[[]]. invariant preserved metadata columns added via metadata_cols.

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
code: data(comma_example_data) # Default: unified output with all four columns annotated <- annotateSites(comma_example_data) si <- siteInfo(annotated) # Sites inside at least one feature (rel_position == 0): sum(sapply(as.list(si$rel_position), function(x) any(x == 0L))) #> [1] 17 # Fractional position of first inside site: inside_idx <- which(lengths(si$frac_position) > 0)[1] si$frac_position[[inside_idx]] #> [1] 0.8857715  # Backward-compatible overlap output (no rel_position / frac_position): ann_ov <- annotateSites(comma_example_data, keep = "overlap") siteInfo(ann_ov)$feature_names[[1]] #> [1] "geneA"

title: commaData: the central data object for the comma package — commaData-class
text: class stores methylation data two assay matrices (accessible via assay): "methylation" Beta values (proportion reads called methylated, range 0–1). Sites coverage min_coverage threshold stored NA. "coverage" Integer read depth site. Per-site metadata rowData(object) includes minimum: chrom, position, strand, mod_type, motif, mod_context. motif column stores sequence context site (e.g., "GATC" "CCWGG") extracted modkit mod_code field. NA Dorado Megalodon callers. mod_context column composite modification type motif (e.g., "6mA_GATC", "5mC_CCWGG"), just mod_type motif unavailable (e.g., "6mA" Dorado/Megalodon data). analyses default running independently per mod_context group prevent spurious mixing biologically distinct methylation events. Per-sample metadata colData(object) includes minimum: sample_name, condition, replicate.

title: commaData: the central data object for the comma package — commaData-class
text: genomeInfo Named integer vector chromosome sizes c(chromosome name = length bp). annotation GRanges genomic features loaded GFF3 BED file. May empty GRanges annotation provided. motifSites GRanges instances user-specified sequence motif genome (e.g., GATC sites). May empty GRanges motif specified.

title: Create a commaData object from methylation calling output files — commaData
text: files Named character vector mapping sample names file paths. Names must match colData$sample_name. Example: c(ctrl_1 = "/path//ctrl_1.bed", treat_1 = "/path//treat_1.bed"). colData data.frame one row per sample. Must contain columns sample_name, condition, replicate. Additional columns (e.g., file_path, batch) preserved. genome Genome size information: named integer vector chromosome sizes (e.g., c(NC_000913 = 4641652L)), path FASTA file, DNAStringSet (Biostrings), BSgenome object. single-chromosome genomes pass BSgenome object directly named integer vector — index BSgenome $ (e.g., BSgenome.Ecoli.NCBI.20080805$NC_000913) yields DNAString chromosome name used. Set NULL omit genome information (recommended). multi-sequence source provided, genomeInfo automatically restricted chromosomes present data. annotation Optional. Path GFF3 BED annotation file, pre-loaded GRanges object. NULL, annotation slot left empty. mod_type Optional character vector specifying modification types retain (e.g., "6mA" c("6mA", "5mC")). NULL, modification types detected files kept. motif Optional character string. DNA sequence motif (e.g., "GATC") locate genome using findMotifSites. results stored motifSites slot genome-wide GRanges motif instances. Requires genome FASTA path BSgenome object (named integer vector). NULL, motifSites slot left empty. Note: argument distinct rowData(object)$motif, stores per-site sequence context extracted automatically modkit mod_code field (e.g., ",GATC,1" → motif = "GATC") NA Dorado Megalodon callers. expected_mod_contexts Named list NULL. provided, specifies modification type / sequence motif combinations retain. Names must modification type strings (e.g., "6mA", "5mC"). Values character vectors motif strings (e.g., c("GATC",   "ACCACC")). Sites whose mod_context (paste(mod_type, motif, sep = "_")) match name–value pair dropped object assembled. message emitted reporting number sites dropped per modification type. Use NULL (default) retain sites. Example: list("6mA" = "GATC", "5mC" = c("CCWGG", "CCGG")). Note: Dorado/Megalodon callers motif NA, mod_context falls back just mod_type (e.g., "6mA"), sites retained include NA motif vector type (e.g., list("6mA" = NA)). min_coverage Integer. Minimum read depth include site. Sites present sample coverage threshold beta value set NA. Sites absent sample entirely also NA. Default 5. caller Character string specifying methylation caller produced input files. One "modkit" (default), "megalodon", "dorado".

title: Identify differentially methylated sites between conditions — diffMethyl
text: input commaData object additional columns rowData: dm_pvalue, dm_padj, dm_delta_beta, one dm_mean_beta_<condition> column per condition level. metadata slot updated analysis parameters result column names.

title: Identify differentially methylated sites between conditions — diffMethyl
text: Default method (method = "methylkit"): Wraps methylKit::calculateDiffMeth(), uses logistic regression SLIM p-value correction. Robust small n, produces calibrated relative p-values suitable downstream filtering. Requires methylKit (BiocManager::install("methylKit")). Alternative model (method = "quasi_f"): per-site quasibinomial GLM empirical Bayes shrinkage dispersion estimates, analogous quasi-likelihood F-test edgeR. Fits quasibinomial GLM per site, collects per-site dispersion \(\hat\phi_j\) residual df \(df_j\), calls squeezeVar compute posterior dispersion estimates \(\{\tilde\phi_j\}\). T-statistics evaluated t-distribution \(d_0 + df_j\) degrees freedom. Requires limma. Alternative model (method = "limma"): Beta values transformed M-values via \(M = \log_2((n_{\mathrm{mod}} + \alpha) / (n_{\mathrm{unmod}} + \alpha))\), lmFit fits OLS model per site eBayes applies empirical Bayes variance shrinkage, borrowing information across sites stabilize per-site variance estimate. Recommended replicates (n < 3 per group). Requires limma (BiocManager::install("limma")). Effect sizes reported original beta scale. Multiple mod contexts: mod_context = NULL (default), modification contexts (mod_type x motif combinations) present object tested independently results combined. Sites belonging tested context receive NA dm_* columns. Testing mod_context rather just mod_type prevents spurious pooling biologically distinct methylation events (e.g., 6mA GATC Dam methyltransferase versus cytosine modifications GATC, likely artefactual). Small-sample note: Differential methylation testing replicates (e.g., n = 1 per group) mathematically possible extremely low statistical power. Treat results exploratory . Result columns added rowData: dm_pvalue Raw p-value GLM Wald test. dm_padj Adjusted p-value (Benjamini-Hochberg default). dm_delta_beta Effect size: mean methylation treatment group minus mean methylation reference (control) group. Positive values indicate hypermethylation treatment. dm_mean_beta_<condition> One column per condition level (named actual condition values), containing per-group mean beta value site. Analysis parameters result column names stored metadata(object)$diffMethyl_params metadata(object)$diffMethyl_result_cols.

title: Identify differentially methylated sites between conditions — diffMethyl
code: data(comma_example_data) # Test for differential 6mA methylation between conditions dm <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition, mod_type = "6mA") #> diffMethyl: testing 'condition' -- 'treatment' vs 'control' (reference) #> methylKit: comparing 'treatment' (treatment) vs 'control' (reference/control) #> uniting...  # How many sites are significant? rd <- as.data.frame(SummarizedExperiment::rowData(dm)) sum(rd$dm_padj < 0.05 & abs(rd$dm_delta_beta) >= 0.2, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 31

title: Parse a Dorado BAM file with MM/ML base modification tags — .parseDorado
text: function reads BAM using scanBam processes MM/ML tags aligned read. MM tag encodes bases carry modifications positions within read sequence (inter-base offsets); parallel ML tag provides modification probabilities (0–255 scaled 0–1). base called modified ML probability \(> 0.5\). Per-site statistics aggregated counting modified reads (\(n_{\text{mod}}\)) total reads genomic position (\(n_{\text{total}}\)). beta value \(\beta = n_{\text{mod}} / n_{\text{total}}\). CIGAR operations used map read positions reference coordinates. Soft-clipped (S) hard-clipped (H) bases excluded; insertions () consume read positions without advancing reference. Recommended workflow: users, simpler first run modkit pileup Dorado BAM, load resulting BED file caller = "modkit". Direct BAM parsing provided users prefer avoid modkit step.

Beta values first transformed M-values: $$M = \log_2\!\left(\frac{n_{\mathrm{mod}} + \alpha}{n_{\mathrm{unmod}} + \alpha}\right)$$ \(\alpha\) pseudocount (default 0.5). M-values approximately normally distributed homoscedastic, making OLS appropriate. linear model fitted across sites simultaneously lmFit. eBayes estimates empirical Bayes prior residual variance across sites computes moderated posterior variance per site — shrinking noisy per-site estimate toward genome-wide mean. P-values derived moderated t-statistic posterior degrees freedom \(d_0 + df_{\mathrm{residual}}\). sites samples non-NA M-values (.e., non-zero coverage min_coverage thresholding) passed limma. Sites NA retain NA result columns. Effect sizes (delta_beta) per-group means reported original beta (0–1) scale interpretability, back-transformed M-value coefficients.

text: methyl_mat Numeric matrix (sites × samples) beta values. NA indicates -coverage sites. coverage_mat Integer matrix (sites × samples) read depths. coldata data.frame least one column matching RHS variable formula (typically condition). formula One-sided formula specifying design (e.g., ~ condition).

title: Per-site quasi-likelihood F-test for differential methylation — .runQuasiF
text: data.frame one row per site (row order methyl_mat), containing: pvalue Moderated quasi-F p-value. NA untestable sites. delta_beta Effect size (treatment mean beta minus reference mean beta) 0–1 scale. mean_beta_<level> One column per condition level containing per-group observed mean beta value.

title: Per-site quasi-likelihood F-test for differential methylation — .runQuasiF
text: limma must installed (listed Suggests). method runs three passes: Pass 1 — per-site GLM. quasibinomial model .betaBinomialTest fitted site: $$\mathrm{glm}(\mathrm{cbind}(n_{\mathrm{mod}},\, n_{\mathrm{unmod}})   \sim \mathrm{condition},\; \mathrm{family} = \mathrm{quasibinomial}())$$ site \(j\), three quantities collected: \(\hat\phi_j = \)fit\$dispersion — Pearson chi-squared dispersion estimate \(df_j = \)fit\$df.residual — residual degrees freedom \(\tilde{t}_j^{(0)} = t_j \times \sqrt{\hat\phi_j}\) — "unscaled" t-statistic (independent \(\hat\phi_j\)), \(t_j\) Wald t-statistic coef(summary(fit)) Pass 2 — empirical Bayes dispersion shrinkage. squeezeVar pools \(\{\hat\phi_j\}\) estimates across testable sites, fits log-normal prior, returns posterior dispersion estimates \(\{\tilde\phi_j\}\) prior degrees--freedom scalar \(d_0\). Pass 3 — moderated test statistic. posterior t-statistic p-value site : $$\tilde{t}_j = \frac{\tilde{t}_j^{(0)}}{\sqrt{\tilde\phi_j}},   \quad p_j = 2\,P(T \leq -|\tilde{t}_j|),\;   T \sim t(d_0 + df_j)$$ additional \(d_0\) degrees freedom power gain unadjusted quasibinomial test. procedure methodologically equivalent quasi-likelihood F-test edgeR (glmQLFTest), adapted methylation proportions (quasibinomial) rather RNA-seq counts (quasi-negative-binomial).

"promoter", "minus_10_signal", "minus_35_signal", "transcription_factor_binding_site" Strip trailing p p\(N\) suffix, split "-" operonic promoters. "protein_binding_site" Target gene transcription_unit attribute (available via metadata_cols) "... {promoter}" pattern. Regulator gene binding-protein name. "RNA_binding_site" Target transcription_unit "regulating {gene}" pattern.  Regulator first word matches gene-name pattern. "terminator" Strip " terminator" suffix.

title: Gene set enrichment analysis of differential methylation results — enrichMethylation
text: method includes "gsea", per-gene score computed site-level statistics genes ranked decreasing order. Three metrics available via score_metric: "combined" (default) \(-\log_{10}(\text{padj}) \times     \text{sign}(\Delta\beta)\); captures significance direction. "padj" \(-\log_{10}(\text{padj})\); significance , direction-agnostic. "delta_beta" \(\Delta\beta\) directly; effect-size ranking. multiple sites map gene, score aggregated across sites using gene_score_agg: "max" (default; selects site largest absolute score, preserving sign) "mean".

title: Gene set enrichment analysis of differential methylation results — enrichMethylation
code: # \donttest{ # Requires clusterProfiler and a custom TERM2GENE mapping if (requireNamespace("clusterProfiler", quietly = TRUE)) {   data(comma_example_data)   dm  <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition, mod_type = "6mA")   ann <- annotateSites(dm, annotation(comma_example_data), keep = "overlap")    # Custom TERM2GENE (works without network access or OrgDb)   fake_t2g <- data.frame(     term = c("PATH:01", "PATH:01", "PATH:02"),     gene = c("geneA",  "geneB",   "geneC")   )   res <- enrichMethylation(ann, TERM2GENE = fake_t2g, method = c("ora", "gsea"))   str(res, max.level = 2) } #>  #> diffMethyl: testing 'condition' -- 'treatment' vs 'control' (reference) #> methylKit: comparing 'treatment' (treatment) vs 'control' (reference/control) #> uniting... #> No gene sets have size between 10 and 500 ... #> --> return NULL... #> using 'fgsea' for GSEA analysis, please cite Korotkevich et al (2019). #> preparing geneSet collections... #> GSEA analysis... #> no term enriched under specific pvalueCutoff... #> List of 2 #>  $ go  :List of 2 #>   ..$ ora : NULL #>   ..$ gsea:Formal class 'gseaResult' [package "DOSE"] with 13 slots #>  $ kegg:List of 2 #>   ..$ ora : NULL #>   ..$ gsea: NULL # }

what: Compute M-values from a commaData object — mValues
title: Compute M-values from a commaData object — mValues
text: Converts per-site beta values (methylation fractions) read depths M-values using pseudocount-offset logit transformation. M-values variance-stabilized relative beta values better suited distance-based analyses PCA hierarchical clustering.

title: Compute M-values from a commaData object — mValues
code: mValues(object, alpha = 0.5, mod_type = NULL, motif = NULL, mod_context = NULL)

title: Compute M-values from a commaData object — mValues
text: object commaData object containing methylation beta values coverage (read depth) assays. alpha Positive numeric scalar. Pseudocount added methylated unmethylated read counts log-transformation. Prevents infinite values beta = 0 beta = 1 corresponds symmetric Beta(alpha, alpha) prior methylation fraction. Default 0.5. mod_type Character string specifying single modification type (e.g., "6mA", "5mC"). NULL (default), M-values computed sites object. motif Character vector NULL. provided, sites matching sequence context motif(s) included. NULL (default), motifs included. mod_context Character vector NULL. provided, sites matching modification context included (e.g., "6mA_GATC"). Applied mod_type motif filters.

title: Compute M-values from a commaData object — mValues
text: numeric matrix M-values dimensions dimnames methylation(object) (subset rows matching mod_type specified). Positive values indicate hypermethylation; negative values indicate hypomethylation; zero corresponds beta value approximately 0.5.

title: Compute M-values from a commaData object — mValues
text: M-value site one sample computed : $$M = \log_2\!\left(\frac{M_{\mathrm{reads}} + \alpha}{U_{\mathrm{reads}} + \alpha}\right)$$ \(M_{\mathrm{reads}} = \mathrm{round}(\beta \times \mathrm{coverage})\) estimated number methylated reads, \(U_{\mathrm{reads}} = \mathrm{coverage} - M_{\mathrm{reads}}\) estimated number unmethylated reads, \(\alpha\) pseudocount offset. Sites zero coverage NA beta values returned NA. pseudocount alpha must strictly positive avoid -Inf NaN values output.

title: Compute M-values from a commaData object — mValues
code: data(comma_example_data)  # Compute M-values for all modification types m <- mValues(comma_example_data) dim(m)          # same as dim(methylation(comma_example_data)) #> [1] 588   6 range(m, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] -4.523562  7.851749  # Only 6mA sites m6 <- mValues(comma_example_data, mod_type = "6mA")  # Use a smaller pseudocount m_tight <- mValues(comma_example_data, alpha = 0.1)

title: PCA of methylation profiles — plot_pca
text: object commaData object. mod_type Character string specifying single modification type (e.g., "6mA", "5mC"). NULL (default), sites modification types used. motif Character vector NULL. provided, sites matching sequence context motif(s) included (e.g., "GATC"). title: PCA of methylation profiles — plot_pca
text: return_data = FALSE (default), ggplot object. PC1 PC2 shown x- y-axes, respectively, percentage variance explained axis labels. point represents one sample labeled sample_name. Points colored color_by. return_data = TRUE, data.frame one row per sample containing columns PC1, PC2, sample_name, columns sampleInfo(object). attribute percentVar (accessible via attr(result, "percentVar")) named numeric vector giving percentage variance explained PC1 PC2.

title: PCA of methylation profiles — plot_pca
text: Beta values first converted M-values via mValues (using alpha = 0.5) PCA. M-values variance-stabilized relative raw beta values, making distance-based analyses reliable especially many sites near 0 1. Sites NA M-values across samples (including sites zero coverage) removed ensure complete data matrix. PCA computed via stats::prcomp centering (center = TRUE) without scaling (scale. = FALSE). warning issued fewer three samples present.

title: PCA of methylation profiles — plot_pca
code: data(comma_example_data) plot_pca(comma_example_data)   # Color by condition, shape by replicate plot_pca(comma_example_data, color_by = "condition")   # Only 6mA sites plot_pca(comma_example_data, mod_type = "6mA")   # Return data for custom plotting d <- plot_pca(comma_example_data, return_data = TRUE) attr(d, "percentVar")  # variance explained by PC1, PC2 #>  PC1  PC2  #> 36.3 17.4

title: TSS-centered methylation profile — plot_tss_profile

Optionally colours sites regulatory feature overlap (e.g., sigma factor binding sites, \(-10\)/\(-35\) elements, transcription factor binding sites).

title: TSS-centered methylation profile — plot_tss_profile
code: plot_tss_profile(   object,   feature_type = "gene",   window = 500L,   regulatory_feature_types = NULL,   mod_type = NULL,   motif = NULL,   mod_context = NULL,   color_by = c("sample", "regulatory_element", "mod_type", "mod_context"),   facet_by = c("none", "sample", "mod_type", "mod_context"),   alpha = 0.4,   show_smooth = FALSE,   smooth_span = 0.3 )

title: TSS-centered methylation profile — plot_tss_profile
text: object commaData object non-empty annotation slot. feature_type Character string specifying feature type whose start coordinate defines TSS (e.g., "gene", "CDS"). Must match value feature_type metadata column annotation(object). Default "gene". window Positive integer. Half-window size base pairs around TSS. Sites beyond window bp upstream downstream every TSS excluded. Default 500L. regulatory_feature_types Character vector NULL. Feature types annotation(object) used label sites color_by = "regulatory_element" (e.g., c("sigma_binding", "promoter_-10", "promoter_-35",   "TF_binding")). Required color_by = "regulatory_element"; ignored otherwise. mod_type Character string NULL. provided, sites specified modification type (e.g., "6mA", "5mC") included. motif Character vector NULL. provided, sites specified sequence motif(s) included. mod_context Character vector NULL. provided, sites whose mod_context rowData column matches one supplied values included. NULL (default), modification contexts included. color_by Character string controlling colour aesthetic: "sample" One colour per sample (default). "regulatory_element" Sites coloured first regulatory feature overlap (regulatory_feature_types); sites overlap labelled "None" shown grey. Requires regulatory_feature_types. "mod_type" One colour per modification type. facet_by Character string controlling optional faceting: "none" (default), "sample", "mod_type". alpha Numeric \((0, 1]\). Point transparency. Default 0.4. show_smooth Logical. TRUE, loess smoothing line overlaid per colour group. Default FALSE. smooth_span Numeric \((0, 1]\). Loess span parameter passed loess. Default 0.3.

title: TSS-centered methylation profile — plot_tss_profile
text: ggplot object. x-axis shows signed position relative TSS base pairs; y-axis shows methylation beta value (0–1). dashed vertical line marks TSS x = 0.

title: TSS-centered methylation profile — plot_tss_profile
text: Unlike plot_metagene, normalises positions \([0, 1]\) averages across sites, plot_tss_profile shows every individual site exact signed base-pair distance nearest TSS (negative = upstream, positive = downstream). preserves absolute spacing promoter elements relative start transcription. Internally calls annotateSites(keep = "proximity") compute signed TSS distances. Strand awareness follows convention: + strand features, position 0 lowest coordinate; - strand features, position 0 highest coordinate (biological TSS). site within window bp multiple TSS features, nearest (absolute distance) used. color_by = "regulatory_element" none specified regulatory_feature_types found annotation(object), function issues message falls back color_by = "sample" plot still renders.

title: comma 0.8.0
text: mod_context rowData column — every commaData object now stores mod_context column rowData combines mod_type motif (e.g. "6mA_GATC", "5mC_CCWGG"). motif NA (Dorado/Megalodon callers), fallback mod_type string alone (never "6mA_NA"). mod_context required enforced setValidity(). modContexts() — new exported S4 accessor returns sorted unique modification context strings commaData object. expected_mod_contexts constructor parameter — commaData() now accepts expected_mod_contexts, named list mapping mod types allowed motifs (e.g. list("6mA" = c("GATC", "ACCACC"), "5mC" = "CCWGG")). Sites unexpected mod_type × motif combinations dropped construction time informative message per mod type. subset(object, mod_context = ...) filter — subset() gains mod_context parameter filtering modification context. diffMethyl() loops mod_context — differential methylation now computed independently mod_context group (e.g. 6mA@GATC 6mA@ACCACC tested separately). mod_context parameter added diffMethyl(), results(), filterResults() context-specific extraction. mod_context parameter added throughout — methylomeSummary(), slidingWindow(), mValues(), writeBED(), eight plot_*() functions gain mod_context = NULL parameter context-level filtering. plot_tss_profile() additionally supports color_by = "mod_context" facet_by = "mod_context".

title: comma 0.8.0
text: Old commaData objects invalid — rowData must include mod_context character column. Objects created earlier versions fail validObject(). Re-create source files using updated constructor.

title: comma 0.6.0
text: mValues() — new exported function converts per-site beta values read depths M-values using formula log2((M_reads + alpha) / (U_reads + alpha)) default pseudocount alpha = 0.5. M-values variance-stabilized relative raw beta values better suited distance-based analyses PCA. plot_pca() now internally transforms beta values M-values via mValues() running PCA, improving separation samples heteroscedastic methylation profiles.

title: comma 0.6.0
text: Version bumped 0.6.0 Bioconductor submission preparation. Package passes R CMD check zero code-level errors warnings. Remaining check notes environment-specific (locale, pdflatex availability) present Bioconductor’s check infrastructure. Full roxygen2 documentation exported functions, including \donttest{} examples throughout.

title: comma 0.5.0
text: plot_methylation_distribution() — beta value density plot per sample, coloured sample name, faceted modification type multiple types present. Useful QC comparing methylation level distributions. plot_genome_track() — genome browser-style scatter plot methylation beta values vs. genomic position. Supports positional windowing via start/ end arguments, mod_type filtering, optional feature annotation rectangles annotation(object). plot_metagene() — average methylation profile across class genomic features, normalized fractional position [0 = TSS, 1 = TTS]. Uses annotateSites(type = "metagene") internally. plot_volcano() — volcano plot differential methylation results. Accepts data.frame output results(). Colors points Hypermethylated, Hypomethylated, significant based user-supplied padj_threshold delta_beta_threshold. plot_heatmap() — geom_tile heatmap top differentially methylated sites (ranked adjusted p-value) across samples. Uses ggplot2 ; ComplexHeatmap dependency required. plot_pca() — PCA per-sample methylation profiles using stats::prcomp(). Points colored optionally shaped column sampleInfo(object). plot_coverage() — histogram sequencing depth per site, per sample. Useful coverage QC differential methylation testing.

title: comma 0.5.0
text: "Getting Started comma" — end--end workflow using comma_example_data. "Working Multiple Modification Types" — joint 6mA + 5mC analysis. Added package-level documentation (?comma) describing overall workflow.

title: comma 0.5.0
text: Added BiocStyle, ComplexHeatmap, ggrepel, patchwork Suggests. None required core functions; patchwork enables combined annotation tracks plot_genome_track() plot_heatmap(). Added VignetteBuilder: knitr DESCRIPTION. Added Visualization biocViews.

title: comma 0.4.0
text: diffMethyl() — core differential methylation function. Accepts commaData object design formula, tests methylation site differential methylation conditions, returns object enriched per-site statistics rowData. Supports two statistical backends: method = "beta_binomial" (default): per-site quasibinomial GLM via base R stats::glm(). extra packages required. method = "methylkit": wraps methylKit::calculateDiffMeth() users prefer methylKit pipeline. Requires methylKit installed. New rowData columns: dm_pvalue, dm_padj (Benjamini-Hochberg default), dm_delta_beta (effect size: treatment minus control mean beta), one dm_mean_beta_<condition> column per condition level. Analysis parameters stored metadata(object)$diffMethyl_params. results() — S4 method extract differential methylation results commaData object tidy data.frame. Supports optional mod_type filtering. filterResults() — S4 method filter results table adjusted p-value (padj) absolute effect size (delta_beta) thresholds. .parseDorado() — full Dorado BAM parser replacing previous stub. Reads MM/ML base modification tags Dorado-aligned BAM files using Rsamtools::scanBam(), parses modification positions CIGAR-decoded read coordinates, aggregates per-read calls per-site beta values. Supports 6mA, 5mC, 4mC BAM file. Invoked automatically commaData(..., caller = "dorado").

title: comma 0.4.0
text: Added methylKit Suggests. Required diffMethyl(..., method = "methylkit").

title: comma 0.3.0
text: annotateSites() — new vectorized annotation function replaces annotateMethylSites(), annotateTSS(), annotateTTS(). Accepts commaData object uses GenomicRanges::findOverlaps() internally — nested -loops. Supports three annotation modes: type = "overlap": assigns feature type name site; non-overlapping sites labelled "intergenic". type = "proximity": reports nearest feature, distance (bp), signed relative position relative feature’s TSS. type = "metagene": reports fractional position (0–1) within overlapping feature, strand-aware. Returns updated commaData new columns rowData. slidingWindow() — new generalised sliding window function replaces methylRollingMedian() methylRollingMean(). Accepts commaData object; genome size always read genome(object) — hardcoded values. Supports stat = "median" stat = "mean", per-sample output, mod_type filtering, circular genome wrap-around. Returns tidy data.frame. methylomeSummary() — new function computes per-sample summary statistics (mean/median/SD beta values, fraction methylated, mean/median coverage). Returns tidy data.frame suitable ggplot2 tabular reporting. Supports mod_type filtering. coverageDepth() — new function bins genome non-overlapping windows computes mean median sequencing depth per window per sample. Replaces calculateMethylSiteDepth(). Returns tidy data.frame. varianceByDepth() — new function computes per-sample methylation variance function sequencing depth. Replaces varByCoverage(). Accepts commaData object; hardcoded column names. Returns tidy data.frame. writeBED() — rewritten scratch. Accepts commaData object, output path, sample name. Removes hardcoded developer paths chromosome names. Writes standard BED9 file optional blue--red itemRGB methylation scale.

title: comma 0.3.0
text: annotateMethylSites(), annotateTSS(), annotateTTS() removed. Use annotateSites() instead. methylRollingMedian() methylRollingMean() removed. Use slidingWindow() instead. calculateMethylSiteDepth() varByCoverage() removed (exported). Use coverageDepth() varianceByDepth() instead.

title: comma 0.2.0
text: Added commaData S4 class (extends SummarizedExperiment) — central data object comma analyses. Stores methylation beta values coverage assay matrices per-site per-sample metadata. Added commaData() constructor: parses one modkit pileup BED files (Megalodon files) merges commaData object consistent site × sample matrices. Supports multi-sample, multi-modification-type data. Added modkit parser (.parseModkit()): reads modkit pileup BED output (primary ONT methylation calling format). Supports three modification types: 6mA (), 5mC (m), 4mC (21839). Added Megalodon parser (.parseMegalodon()): reads legacy Megalodon per-read BED output backward compatibility existing datasets. Added Dorado BAM parser stub (.parseDorado()): yet implemented; provides clear error message directing users use modkit pileup first. Added findMotifSites(): locates instances DNA sequence motif (e.g., GATC, CCWGG) genome (BSgenome object FASTA file) returns GRanges positions strands. Added loadAnnotation(): reads GFF3 BED annotation files returns GRanges standardized feature_type name metadata columns. Added accessor functions: methylation(), coverage(), sampleInfo(), siteInfo(), modTypes(), genome(), annotation(), motifSites(). Added subsetting: [ sites/samples subset() filtering mod_type, condition, chrom. Added internal genome utilities: .validateGenomeInfo(), .circularIndex(), .makeSeqinfo().

title: comma 0.2.0

Run data-raw/create_example_data.R regenerate. Added inst/extdata/example_modkit.bed: small modkit pileup BED constructor tests examples. Added inst/extdata/example.gff3: matching GFF3 annotation example genome.

title: comma 0.2.0
text: Package renamed CoMMA comma (Comparative Methylomics Microbial Analysis). new name reflects broader scope — modification types (6mA, 5mC, 4mC), just adenine methylation. Version bumped 0.2.0. R (>= 4.1.0) now minimum R version requirement. Added biocViews field preparation Bioconductor submission.
